task_text: We're about to kick off Q3 planning. Write a one-page risk summary that identifies the top risks for the Q3 roadmap and maps each risk to the current project situation. Save it as q3_risk_summary.txt.

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

input.content:
# Q3 2026 Risk Summary: Project Helios

## Overview
As we kick off Q3 planning for Project Helios, several critical risks have been identified that could impact upcoming milestones, including system integration, the July 25 product demo, August UAT, and the October 1 target launch date. This document outlines the top risks from our risk register, mapped directly to the current project situation.

## Top Risks & Current Situation Mapping

1. Data Quality and Ingestion Blockers (Risk ID: R-01)
   - Risk Score: 9 (High Likelihood, High Impact)
   - Current Project Situation: The data ingestion module is currently 85% complete, but development is actively blocked by inconsistent date formats in the Meridian source data (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY). The dev team is currently awaiting clarification from Meridian IT, which is causing an estimated 2-3 day delay in Sprint 14. 
   - Q3 Roadmap Impact: Threatens the impending System Integration completion milestone (target: July 18) and the live data ingestion demonstration scheduled for the July 25 product demo. 

2. UAT Timeline Slippage & Contract Penalties (Risk ID: R-04)
   - Risk Score: 6 (High Likelihood, Medium Impact)
   - Current Project Situation: User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for the Meridian Group is scheduled for August 17-28. However, a 1-week schedule extension has already been consumed earlier in the project. 
   - Q3 Roadmap Impact: Any further UAT slippage of more than 2 weeks will push the final launch date (October 1) into Q4. Crucially, this would trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review per the Meridian contract. 

3. Third-Party License Renewal Delay (Risk ID: R-02)
   - Risk Score: 6 (Medium Likelihood, High Impact)
   - Current Project Situation: The third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal is still pending. It is currently under legal review with a target resolution date of July 14.
   - Q3 Roadmap Impact: If the license is not secured promptly, the team will be unable to finalize client dashboard v1 (target: July 22) or successfully execute the dashboard visualization segment of the July 25 product demo (required per contract Section 9.3).

4. Dashboard Performance Hardening (Risk ID: R-03)
   - Risk Score: 6 (Medium Likelihood, High Impact)
   - Current Project Situation: Dashboard performance on large datasets remains an active risk, and the critical benchmark task (HEL-247) has not yet started. The client dashboard itself is only 60% complete.
   - Q3 Roadmap Impact: The Q3 roadmap specifies a load time target of <2 seconds for 100k records during the August-September Performance Hardening phase. Delayed benchmarking limits the time available to resolve potential architectural bottlenecks before the final production launch.

## Next Steps & Mitigations
- Escalation: Expedite communications with Meridian IT to resolve the data format inconsistencies immediately.
- Legal Follow-up: Ensure the Legal team prioritizes the GeoSync license renewal for clearance by July 14 to avoid demo delays.
- Concurrent Benchmarking: Assign resources to begin dashboard performance benchmarking (HEL-247) in parallel with finalizing the dashboard v1 module.
- Strict Scope/Timeline Management: Strictly manage the remaining timeline and tightly control Q3 feature additions (e.g., self-service mapping editor) to protect the UAT dates and prevent triggering Section 7.1 contract penalties.

content.text:
I have reviewed the current project materials, specifically the Q3 roadmap, risk register, and the latest status update for Project Helios. 

I've drafted a one-page risk summary that identifies the top risks (data quality/ingestion blockers, UAT timeline slippage, software license renewal, and dashboard performance) and clearly maps them to the current project situation. 

The document has been saved to your working directory as `q3_risk_summary.txt`.
